strengths: Novel integration of physical BRDF modeling and MRI-inspired angular frequency analysis to address non-Lambertian depth estimation.
Comprehensive evaluation across multiple challenging light field datasets, including specific non-Lambertian and mixed-material scenarios.
Clear identification of the texture-copying failure mode in existing EPI networks, providing strong motivation for the proposed dual-mask approach.
weaknesses: The mathematical formalization relies on trivial operations (e.g., grayscale conversion, bilinear interpolation) rather than deep theoretical derivations for the core physical models.
The reference list appears sparse (fewer than 30 unique citations identified in the text), lacking comprehensive coverage of recent state-of-the-art literature.
Inconsistent reporting of primary evaluation metrics across sections (MAE emphasized in Abstract/Introduction, while MSE and Bad Pixel ratios are highlighted in the Conclusion).
key_gaps_vs_tcsvt: Insufficient citation count and depth; TCSVT expects exhaustive surveys of related video/image technology literature (typically 50+ references).
Methodological rigor needs enhancement; the mathematical formulation of the BRDF and angular FFT modules must be explicitly detailed with proper equation environments.
Metric consistency and completeness; standard TCSVT papers require uniform reporting of MAE, MSE, and BadPix across all datasets and ablation studies without cut-off text.
